name: Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza

content.verdict: The Nile Plaza is the most reliable luxury bed in Cairo — a Garden City high-rise where every room has a Nile-facing balcony and the eight restaurants do most of the city's heavy lifting on their own. For honeymooners using Cairo as a launchpad to the Pyramids and Egyptian Museum, this is the calm, polished base where the noise of the city stays outside.
content.best_room: Book a Premier Nile View room minimum, but the right honeymoon move is a One-Bedroom Nile View Suite — wraparound balcony, deep tub with Nile view, and access to the Royal Club Lounge with continuous food and drink service throughout the day. Even-numbered floors above 18 give the cleanest view past the rooftop tanks of neighbouring buildings.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival, Nile sunset, & Zitouni dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Cairo (CAI), the hotel sends a car for the 45-minute transfer through evening traffic. Settle into your Nile View Suite, take an hour on the balcony watching the river boats light up, then head down to Zitouni for refined Egyptian cuisine — the molokhia and the slow-cooked lamb fattah are the right introduction. Early night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pyramids of Giza & Sphinx with private Egyptologist
itinerary_7_nights.description: Early start (6:30am breakfast) to beat the heat and crowds — private guide and car to Giza Plateau, 4 hours covering Khufu, Khafre, Menkaure, the Great Sphinx, and the Solar Boat Museum. Optional inside-the-pyramid ticket for the Khufu chamber. Back at the hotel by 1pm for a long pool day on Floor 8 with mezze lunch, then dinner at Bella for handmade pasta and a Tuscan wine list.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Egyptian Museum & Khan El Khalili souk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the new Grand Egyptian Museum at Giza (or the historic Tahrir Square museum if scheduling) for Tutankhamun's treasures and the royal mummy gallery. Lunch at Naguib Mahfouz café in the Khan El Khalili souk, then an unhurried wander through the alleys for spices, alabaster, and antique perfume bottles. Dinner at the rooftop 8 — Cairo's skyline at night is exceptional from a 30th-floor terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Coptic Cairo & Islamic Cairo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slower-paced cultural day — morning in Coptic Cairo for the Hanging Church, Ben Ezra Synagogue, and the Coptic Museum, then a falafel-and-koshari lunch at a local spot. Afternoon in Islamic Cairo — Sultan Hassan Mosque and Al-Rifa'i side by side, then up to the Saladin Citadel for the panoramic view across the city to the Pyramids. Hammam ritual at the spa to recover, dinner room-service on the balcony.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to Saqqara & Memphis
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private day trip 30 minutes south to Saqqara — the Step Pyramid of Djoser, the Pyramid of Unas with its pyramid texts, and the painted tombs at the necropolis are far quieter than Giza and arguably more rewarding. Lunch at Sakkara Country Club. Afternoon at Memphis (the Ramses colossus and the alabaster sphinx) before returning by 4pm. Pool, then dinner at 8 again for the rooftop sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Felucca on the Nile & Zamalek dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Easy morning at the spa for couples' massage. Mid-afternoon, the concierge arranges a private felucca from the Maadi corniche — bring a bottle of cold wine, drift past Manial Palace, watch the sun set behind the Cairo Tower. Dinner across the river in the Zamalek district at Sequoia for a Nile-side mezze evening with shisha and live oud.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast & late checkout
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long breakfast at the Royal Club Lounge with the Nile through the window, an hour by the pool, then a final shopping pass through the hotel's Khan El Khalili shop or the nearby First Mall for bookable extras. Late checkout (3pm typically granted) before transfer to the airport for evening flights to Europe or the Red Sea for a beach extension.

caveats: It is a city hotel — there is no beach, and pool space is limited compared to Red Sea resorts. Pair with a Sharm or Hurghada extension if you want a true beach honeymoon.
Cairo traffic is heavy and unpredictable; everything takes longer than Google Maps says. Build buffer into every excursion and use the hotel's car service rather than ride-hails.
The hotel hosts business travellers and weddings; weekend public spaces (Thursday and Friday nights) can feel busy — request a Royal Club room for the calmer lounge.
Air pollution and dust are real in Cairo; honeymooners with respiratory sensitivity should pack accordingly and consider a half-day inside the spa.
Some restaurants close during Ramadan daytimes; if travelling in Ramadan, confirm dining schedules at booking and embrace the iftar feasts at sunset.

faqs.question: Is it good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes for couples who want Cairo's history with luxury polish — the Nile-view suites, eight restaurants, and reliable car service to all the major sites make it the easiest base in the city. Honeymoon perks include sparkling wine, room upgrade based on availability, and a complimentary spa ritual on stays of four nights or more.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: October to April — Cairo summer (June through September) is brutally hot and dusty. November to early March is the sweet spot, with daytime temperatures in the low 20s and clear skies.

faqs.question: Should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Royal Club Nile View Suites should be booked 2–3 months ahead for the December–February high season. Standard rooms are usually available 4–6 weeks out outside Easter and Christmas weeks.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, it is a full-service city hotel that welcomes families and business travellers. The Royal Club floors and 30th-floor 8 restaurant feel adult and refined; honeymooners gravitate there.

faqs.question: Best room type for couples?
faqs.answer: A One-Bedroom Nile View Suite with Royal Club access is the honeymoon pick — wraparound balcony, lounge food and drink throughout the day, and the cleanest river view.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Cairo International Airport (CAI) is forty-five minutes from the hotel by private car (longer in rush hour). Direct flights from most European, Gulf, African, and US East Coast hubs. The hotel arranges fast-track immigration and meet-and-greet on request.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza

Dear team,

We are planning our honeymoon and are interested in staying from [DATE] to [DATE]. Thank you,
[Names]
